name: Pineapple Chicken
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Pineapple chunks plus juice (enough to make 1 cup)
1 1/2 tbsp Corn starch, dissolved in 1/2 cup Water
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice chicken into pieces measuring approximately 1 inch by 2 inches by 1/4 inch. Dredge the chicken in corn starch and set aside to dry slightly on wax paper.
2. Prepare a batter in a small bowl consisting of 1 egg, 1/2 cup water, 1/2 cup flour, and salt. Mix the ingredients thoroughly until smooth.
3. Pour oil halfway up in your wok. Heat the burner to high and allow the oil to heat up. Test the readiness of the oil by placing a piece of bread crust into it. If the bread crust turns medium brown, the oil is ready. If the bread crust turns dark brown or black, turn off the heat and let the oil cool for 5 minutes or more before turning the heat back on. Retest the oil before cooking anything in it.
4. When the oil is ready, place about 8-10 pieces of chicken into the batter. Using chopsticks or a fork, lift the pieces out of the batter and slice them into the hot oil in the wok. The chicken pieces will initially slide to the bottom of the oil and then rise. After the chicken has risen, deep fry for 2 1/2 minutes on each side. Drain the chicken on paper towels to remove excess oil. Place the chicken on a serving plate. Repeat the procedure until all the chicken is cooked.
5. In a separate small pot, combine 1/2 cup brown sugar, 1/4 cup cider vinegar. Cook until the brown sugar and vinegar are dissolved. Add pineapple chunks and juice. Stir thoroughly. Mix corn starch with 1/2 cup water in a separate bowl. Add this mixture to the pot and stir thoroughly. Cook on medium heat for 3-5 minutes until the gravy is clear. Add maraschino cherries. Pour the gravy over the chicken.
NOTE: Breaded, deep-fried shrimp can also be used in this recipe.
